core_model.structure_summary: A continuous-time Markov jump process of binary site variables on a Cartesian lattice, where each inactive site switches on irreversibly at a rate that is a constant plus a term proportional to the number of active nearest neighbors.
core_model.canonical_form: X_j(t) \in \{0,1\}, \quad \mathbb{P}(X_j(t+\Delta t)=1 \mid X_j(t)=0) = \big(p + \tfrac{q}{2D}\textstyle\sum_{k\sim j} X_k(t)\big)\Delta t, \quad X_j: 0 \to 1 \text{ irreversible}

variables.meaning: binary indicator at each lattice site (inactive/active)
lattice site index / position on the Cartesian grid

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A first-order relaxation for the site (or aggregate) activation fraction toward full activation, driven by a constant term plus a term proportional to neighboring activation, with boundary sites relaxing more slowly.

notes: Core is the stochastic discrete Bass model (jump process on a lattice, irreversible 0->1 transitions with neighbor-dependent rate). The mean-field/expectation reduction gives the deterministic adoption-fraction dynamics f(t) satisfying a first-order ODE (the 1D closed form 1-exp(...)), which is the skeleton; boundary vs central node distinction is the paper's main structural result.
